common.tagline: Let us know our Tamil Nadu

footer.mission: Arivom is an open-source, ad-free civic data platform for the people of Tamil Nadu. Every fact with its source.
footer.neutrality: This platform is not affiliated with any political party. No opinions. Sourced facts only.

hero.title: Know your neighbourhood, know your representatives
hero.subtitle: Clear, sourced information on Tamil Nadu's 234 assembly and 39 parliamentary constituencies. No ads, no fees, no slant.

transparency.title: Every fact carries its source
transparency.body: Every fact here arrives with its origin, retrieval date, and collection method, one tap away. A fact that cannot carry a source does not enter Arivom.

neutrality.title: No leaning toward any party
neutrality.body: No opinions, no scores, no labels. We present sourced facts. The conclusions are yours to draw.

craft.title: A calm, fast experience
craft.body: No ads, no tracking, no infinite scroll. Built to run fast on a basic phone over a slow connection.

status.title: What is available now?
status.body: Arivom is just taking shape. Basic details for every constituency are available today. Coming in the next stages: your representatives, vacancies and by-election status, your district's news, and education and health indicators.
status.note: You can always see when each dataset was last updated on the <link>data status</link> page.

feed.onboardSub: Add your places once. Your civic feed starts there. Saved on this device only, no account needed.
feed.manageHint: Manage your places in the More tab.
feed.quiet: No recent stories for this district. Statewide news is below.
feed.followHint: Follow representatives to see their stories here.

locate.title: Find your constituency
locate.intro: Share your location and we will show your assembly and parliamentary constituencies. Or search by name below.
locate.denied: Location permission was denied, and that is fine. You can search by your area's name below.
locate.failed: Could not determine your location. Try searching by name below.
locate.outside: This point appears to be outside Tamil Nadu's constituency boundaries. You can search for a Tamil Nadu constituency by name below.
locate.accuracyNote: Constituency boundaries follow the 2008 delimitation order. If you are very close to a boundary, check the neighbouring constituency too.
locate.privacyNote: Your location is used only to find the constituency. It is never stored.
locate.pickerHint: Type a constituency or district name, in Tamil or English.

vacancies.title: Vacancies and by-elections
vacancies.intro: Assembly constituencies that are currently vacant, with their by-election status. This page is checked daily.
vacancies.methodNote: Changes appear in this list only after they are verified against sources. No status is ever changed automatically.

constituencies.title: Constituencies of Tamil Nadu
constituencies.subtitle: 234 assembly constituencies · 39 parliamentary constituencies
constituencies.searchPlaceholder: Search in Tamil or English (e.g. Madurai)
constituencies.noResults: No matching constituency. Try a different spelling.
constituencies.emptyState: Constituency data has not been imported yet. Once the data pipelines run, all 234 assembly and 39 parliamentary constituencies will appear here.

representatives.title: Representatives
representatives.emptyState: Representative details are not connected yet. Election results and candidate details arrive in the next milestone. We never display guessed or placeholder information as real.
representatives.nameTaPending: The Tamil rendering of this name is pending source verification. We do not use machine transliterations.
representatives.wardEmpty: Ward-level (local body) representative details are not connected yet. They begin with the Madurai Corporation pilot. Your MLA and MP are shown above.

affidavit.title: Self-declared details
affidavit.framing: Declared by the candidate to the Election Commission in their nomination affidavit. Not independently verified. Source: ECI affidavit, via MyNeta (ADR).
affidavit.pending: This representative's affidavit has not yet been analyzed on MyNeta (ADR). It will appear here once published.

result.title: Election result
result.figuresNote: Vote counts follow the preliminary data entered by returning officers. They will be checked against the final Form 20 record once published. The outcome itself and the government are settled.

vacancy.title: This constituency's seat is currently vacant
vacancy.body: The seat became vacant when {member} resigned on {date}.
vacancy.byElection: A by-election announcement from the Election Commission is awaited.

aboutData.title: About this data
aboutData.body: The information on this page is collected automatically from government sources. A structured way to report an error is coming soon.

contact.title: Contact details
contact.pending: Officially published contact details (office address, phone, email) are not connected yet. They will appear here once the official assembly and parliament directories are available.
contact.policy: We show only officially published contact channels. We never publish personal numbers.

constituency.placesFull: Your places list is full (5). Remove one in More.

education.title: Education
education.ptrNote: How many students there are for each teacher at that school level.
education.sourceNote: Schools upload this data themselves to UDISE+, the education ministry's national system. It is not independently verified. Counts are as of 30 September of each academic year.
education.unavailable: Education data for this district is not published yet. It will appear here when UDISE+ publishes it.
education.infraIntro: Out of {total} schools. Each row shows how many have that facility.

health.title: Health

indicators.improvedDrinkingWater: Households with an improved drinking water source
indicators.healthInsurance: Households with health insurance or a health scheme
indicators.institutionalBirth: Births in hospitals or health facilities
indicators.ancFourVisits: Mothers with at least 4 antenatal check-ups
indicators.stunted: Children under 5 who are stunted (low height for age)
indicators.wasted: Children under 5 who are wasted (low weight for height)
indicators.anaemicChildren: Children aged 6 to 59 months with anaemia

health.sourceNote: NFHS-5 is a sample survey of households run in 2019-21 by the Union health ministry with IIPS. District figures are estimates from a sample and carry uncertainty.
health.unavailable: NFHS-5 (2019-21) does not publish a separate factsheet for this district. The survey used the district list from before 2019.

groupNotes.nutrition: These numbers show how common each condition is in the survey sample.

water.title: Drinking water
water.missionLine: Jal Jeevan Mission, cumulative as on {date}
water.coverageLabel: Rural households with a tap water connection
water.certified: Har Ghar Jal certified: village assemblies have certified that every rural household here has a tap connection.
water.sourceNote: These figures are reported by the implementing agencies into the mission's records. They are administrative counts, not a survey.
water.unavailable: The Jal Jeevan Mission tracks rural households only and publishes no data for this district.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.intro: How Arivom works, where the data comes from, and what we will never do. All documented here, in the open.

transparency.title: 1. Transparency
transparency.body: Every fact is stored with its source, retrieval date, collection method, and confidence. A fact that cannot carry a source cannot even be inserted into our database. This is enforced in software, not just policy.

neutrality.title: 2. Political neutrality
neutrality.body: We do not stamp any news outlet with a 'bias label'; we do not give any locality a single composite score; no opinion of ours appears anywhere. Candidate asset declarations and the like are always marked 'self-declared'.

craft.title: 3. Careful design
craft.body: It must run fast on an ordinary phone over 4G. Tamil typography must be excellent. It must be usable by everyone (WCAG AA). These are our quality targets.

spine.title: Representatives and elections
spine.results: Winners and vote figures come from the Election Commission's results portal. Names are matched across languages by anchoring on the vote counts, never by transliterating names. Vote figures carry a provisional label until the Commission's final publication.
spine.affidavits: Asset, criminal case and education details come from candidates' own election affidavits, as analysed by the Association for Democratic Reforms (MyNeta). They are always labelled self-declared. Every import re-checks at least twenty candidates against the source pages and stops on any mismatch.
spine.vacancies: A seat's vacancy status changes only through a human-confirmed record with a citation. Automated monitors raise signals for review; they can never change a status on their own.

separation.title: Data and opinions never mix
separation.body: Data indicators come from published datasets. Community sentiment, when it arrives, will be shown separately with its sample size, and only above a minimum number of ratings. The two are never combined into one number. No composite scores exist anywhere on this platform.

currentData.title: Current data sources
currentData.body: Two datasets are connected today: the Tamil Nadu administrative hierarchy from the Government of India's Local Government Directory (LGD), and the Election Commission of India's constituency list. Tap the 'Source' chip on any page to see the details.

education.title: Education indicators (UDISE+)
education.source: District education data comes from UDISE+, the school information system of the Union education ministry. Schools upload this data themselves once a year, with 30 September as the reference date. It is not independently verified. Every education panel carries a "Reported by schools" label and a source chip.
education.computed: We compute school-level groups from class-wise enrollment: pre-primary, classes 1 to 5, classes 6 to 8, classes 9 and 10, and classes 11 and 12. State figures are the sum of district figures. Every import checks our district sums against the state totals UDISE+ publishes separately. The import stops if they do not match.
education.withheld: UDISE+ also publishes enrollment ratios and dropout rates. We do not show them yet. We could not verify how their level groups are computed, and a ratio we cannot explain can mislead. They will appear once we can document their computation.
education.facilities: Facility figures count schools that report the facility in working condition. Percentages are out of all schools in the district for that year. One education district, Chennai (extended GCC area), exists only in UDISE+ and is counted in state totals only.

health.title: Health indicators (NFHS-5)
health.source: District health data comes from round 5 of the National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5), run in 2019-21 by the Union health ministry with IIPS. It is a sample survey of households. District figures are estimates and carry uncertainty. Every health panel says so and carries a source chip.
health.computed: We display selected percentages from the published district factsheets as they are. Nothing is computed or combined by us.
health.withheld: Two published indicators are not shown yet. Vaccination coverage arrives damaged in the data.gov.in mirror we read. Sex ratio at birth swings widely in district samples and is easy to misread. Both will appear once verified against the official factsheet documents.
health.districts: The survey used the district list from before 2019, so 32 districts have factsheets. The six districts created later were surveyed as part of their parent districts and show an honest note instead of data.

water.title: Drinking water (Jal Jeevan Mission)
water.source: District water figures come from the Jal Jeevan Mission dashboard run by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti. Implementing agencies report progress into the mission's records; the dashboard publishes cumulative district totals. We read those totals as they are.
water.computed: We display the mission's published coverage percentage and the two counts behind it. We compute nothing; a check on every import confirms the percentage matches the counts.
water.scope: The mission covers rural households only. Chennai is fully urban and has no data here. 'Har Ghar Jal certified' means village assemblies have certified full coverage. A reported figure without certification is the agency's own claim.
water.caveat: These are administrative counts, not a survey. They show reported infrastructure coverage, not water quality or supply hours.

stories.title: How stories are chosen
stories.intro: The news feed is selected by subject, never by who a story favours. The rules are published here in full.
stories.pool: We track a fixed registry of Tamil Nadu news outlets and store headlines and links only, never article text. Clearly non-civic sections (cinema, sports, astrology, video galleries) are dropped at collection, before anything is stored.
stories.classification: Each stored item is classified by its subject: civic (governance, courts, elections, the legislature, public services, public safety, policy), adjacent (economy, employment, education, health, environment, weather, infrastructure, prices), or soft (entertainment, celebrity, sports, astrology, viral items). Feeds show civic and adjacent items only. Soft items never appear in the product; they stay in the database for coverage analysis, and their count is shown on the data status page.
stories.priority: Some stories carry a priority chip. It is applied when the subject has statewide policy impact, concerns elections or by-elections, court rulings, legislature decisions, or public safety affecting many people. The judgement is about the subject, never about who it favours.
stories.differ: When outlets report conflicting facts about the same event, the story carries a "sources differ" marker and the summary names the disagreement with citations. We never compute controversy scores.
stories.titles: Displayed headlines are written by our pipeline in a calm, informative voice: no exclamation marks, no teasers, no sensational words. The outlet's original headline stays one tap away on the story page.
stories.actorBlind: Within the feed nothing is boosted or buried by party or person. A weekly sample of displayed titles and summaries is reviewed by a human against these same rules.
stories.interim: Story analysis has not started yet. Until it does, feeds show original headlines in your language, and no stored item is excluded by classification.

corrections.title: Corrections
corrections.body: A structured form for reporting incorrect information is coming soon. Every accepted correction will be published in a public corrections log alongside the original value.

methodology.wip: This document grows with the platform. As each new dataset is connected, its computation method will be explained here in detail.

freshness.title: How fresh is the data?
freshness.intro: See when each data source was last retrieved. This page is generated directly from our database. It is never updated by hand.
freshness.emptyState: No data has been imported yet. Once the data pipelines start running, this table will fill in automatically.

pool.title: The story pool
pool.body: News items collected: {total}. Excluded from feeds as non-civic: {soft}. Awaiting analysis: {unclassified}. The selection rules are on the methodology page.

sla.legend: The status compares each source's last successful check with its checking schedule. The time shows when we last checked, not when the source last published.
sla.thresholdsBody: News feeds are checked every 30 minutes: overdue after 3 hours, stopped after 24. The vacancy watch runs daily: overdue after 2 days, stopped after 4. Monthly imports: overdue after 45 days, stopped after 75. Sources checked on demand carry no colour.

corrections.title: Corrections
corrections.intro: Every accepted correction to something Arivom displayed, with the original value kept. Mistakes are part of the record, not deleted from it.
corrections.empty: No corrections have been needed yet.
corrections.note: Each entry keeps what was shown before, what it became, when, and why. When accounts arrive, corrections you report from any source chip will be reviewed and, if accepted, appear here.

rightToKnow.title: Your right to know
rightToKnow.intro: Everything on this platform is public information. Where the data stops, that edge is itself a fact worth knowing: who holds the information, and what the law says about your right to it.

law.title: What the law says
law.act: The Right to Information Act, 2005 gives every citizen the right to request information from public authorities. The authority must reply, normally within 30 days.
law.proactive: Section 4 of the Act goes further: public authorities must publish key information on their own, without anyone asking. That includes their functions, budgets, and the particulars of decisions that affect the public.
law.meaning: Much of what you see here exists because of these duties. Where a panel on this platform says data is unavailable, the right usually still exists; the publication has not happened yet.

edges.title: Where the edges are today
edges.intro: These are places where this platform has reached the current limits of what government publishes in usable form.

wards.title: Ward councillors
wards.body: The State Election Commission publishes local body results as documents that are hard to reuse, not as data. Ward-level pages here will begin with a Madurai Corporation pilot built from those documents.

contacts.title: Official contact details of representatives
contacts.body: No official, regularly updated public directory of MLA and MP office contacts is available in reusable form. Contact sections stay empty rather than showing unverified numbers.

edges.tracking: Every source this platform checks, and how fresh it is, is listed openly.

ask.title: How a citizen can ask
ask.how: Any citizen can file an RTI application with the public information officer of the department that holds the records. A small fee applies; no reason needs to be given.
ask.appeal: If the reply does not come or is refused, the Act provides appeals: first within the authority, then to the Information Commission.

rightToKnow.note: This page states what the law provides, in plain words. It is information, not legal advice, and this platform is not affiliated with any party or campaign.
rightToKnow.edgeLink: This is information the government is meant to publish. Your right to know

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or may have moved.

government.title: Government of Tamil Nadu
government.intro: The current council of ministers, their portfolios, and the assembly's party composition.
government.compositionNote: Computed from the active members in our records. Vacant seats are shown separately.
government.officialNote: Official department websites and ministers' office contact details will be connected once the government's official directory is available.
government.departmentsNote: A minister's combined portfolios are shown split by department. Department names appear as the source records them.

deptNews.title: {department} · news
deptNews.empty: No stories are tagged with this department yet. Story analysis has not started; this page fills in on its own once it does.
deptNews.note: Stories are matched to departments by name. The match is approximate until the official department directory becomes available.

government.filterPlaceholder: Type a department or minister's name…
government.filterEmpty: Nothing matches. Try a shorter word.

news.title: News
news.intro: Stories from tracked Tamil Nadu outlets, grouped by event. Summaries are written by our automated pipeline in plain words and checked before publishing. Every sentence cites its sources. We store only headlines and links, never full articles.
news.emptyDistrict: No recent stories are placed in this district yet. Statewide stories are shown below.
news.empty: No stories yet. News collection has just started. Check back soon.
news.singleSource: Reported by one tracked outlet so far
news.aiNote: This summary was written automatically from the cited reporting. It was checked for accuracy and neutrality before publishing.
news.summaryPending: A checked summary is not ready yet. The headlines below come from the outlets themselves.
news.coverageNote: This table only shows which tracked outlets reported this story. It says nothing about why. We do not rate or label outlets.

lockedReasons.communal: Discussion is closed on this story because it touches communal tensions. The facts stay visible.
lockedReasons.sub_judice: Discussion is closed on this story because the matter is before a court. The facts stay visible.
lockedReasons.allegations: Discussion is closed on this story because it centres on unverified allegations against a person. The facts stay visible.

news.methodNote: Stories are grouped by event using shared names, places and dates. Each sentence of a summary carries a number like [1] pointing to its source. Full articles are never stored or republished. Read more on the methodology page.
news.coverageIntro: How the tracked outlets that reported this story each tell it. Descriptions only. No ratings, no labels.
news.detailPending: The detailed summary and coverage notes for this story are being prepared. The outlets' own reporting is below.
news.inNumbersIntro: Figures from our own sourced records that this story touches.
news.reportIssue: Report an issue with this story

brief.title: Today in Tamil Nadu
brief.pending: The daily brief starts once story clustering is running.

more.title: More
more.noPlaces: No places added yet. Find your constituency to start your feed.

knowledgeMap.deviceNote: Which pages you have seen is remembered only on this device.
